title: Bridge Paper: Neural Correlates of Metacognition Impairment in Opioid Addiction.
author: Frontier Science / Hermes T2

body.text: This bridge paper connects the esoteric/philosophical concept of **Dashboard Diagnosis** with mechanistic science from PubMed/MEDLINE.
**Bridge thesis:** Metacognition — self-monitoring, metacognitive awareness, prefrontal-parietal insight circuits.

Paper: Neural Correlates of Metacognition Impairment in Opioid Addiction.
DOI: 10.1016/j.bpsc.2024.07.014
Abstract: BACKGROUND: Individuals with substance use disorder show impaired self-awareness of ongoing behavior. This deficit suggests problems with metacognition, which has been operationalized in the cognitive neuroscience literature as the ability to monitor and evaluate the success of one's own cognition and behavior. However, the neural mechanisms of metacognition have not been characterized in a population with drug addiction. METHODS: Community samples of participants with opioid use disorder (OUD)

notes: Auto-acquired from PubMed/MEDLINE on 2026-07-11. Phase 1 (Dashboard Diagnosis).
